summary: A medieval-style castle or fortress structure with four tall corner towers topped with stepped/tiered roofs, connected by lower perimeter walls. The interior courtyard is open with a dirt floor. The building features dark stone construction with regular window openings and is surrounded by a decorative stone foundation.

shape.overall_form: rectangular fortress with four corner towers and connecting walls forming a courtyard

rebuild_hints: Build four tall corner towers approximately 12 blocks high, positioned at each corner of a 16x16 base
Connect towers with lower perimeter walls (approximately 6-7 blocks high) on all four sides
Create stepped/tiered roof structures on each tower using wood slabs and dark oak, with 2-3 tiers per tower
Add regular window openings (approximately 2x2 blocks) on each tower face, spaced evenly
Place stone foundation blocks around the entire perimeter as decorative base
Leave the interior courtyard open with dirt/brown block flooring
Ensure bilateral symmetry across both horizontal axes
Use stonebrick as primary material for all walls and towers
uncertainties: Exact interior floor composition unclear from exterior views
Precise number of interior floors and room divisions not visible
Exact dimensions of towers and walls estimated from visual proportions
Specific wood type for roof elements uncertain; appears to be dark oak or similar dark wood
Interior courtyard details and any structures within it not clearly visible from provided angles
